session_logs.summary: War Time Hour 0 — War Tracker init. Imported 7-snippet war-prep chronicles package into Codex (1 volume + 12 chapters + 16 lore + 9 schisms + 1 session record); flagged three schema mismatches (journal date-grouped not flat; lore rationale↔body; schism v2 vs v1 shape). Drafted canon-inst-003 formalizing Ignis's Temple Builder seat + Minister: Output vacation (queued for Sovereign hand with cluster-assignment marked PENDING_SOVEREIGN). Drafted session_log snippet-handler spec (consumer of SESSION_V2.md) for Orinth implementation, unblocking Lyra's first telemetry drop. Drafted two missing Hour-0 briefings (Dashboard Phase 1–2 for Nyx+Sovereign; Invoicing Phase 1 for Theron+Solara). Verified Temple of Mars GH Pages serving at 200; Codex raw CDN endpoints all 200; Temple fetcher config confirmed reading `main` branch — Temple will reflect these imports on merge.
decisions: Snippets 01–07 from 2026-04-23-temple-war-prep-chronicles applied via direct Python write (Codex snippet-import UI was not invoked because the existing dispatcher does not yet recognize the `{snippet_type, operations}` envelope used by the war-prep package). Future war-prep snippets should use the legacy `{_snippet_version, <type_key>}` envelope OR wait for operations-envelope bridge in the session_log handler work.

Schema mismatch #1: journal.json is date-grouped `[{date, sessions[]}]`, not a flat `sessions[]` as SESSION_V2 implies. Session 07 routed into the existing 2026-04-23 date group via `started_at.slice(0,10)`. The session_log handler spec makes this routing explicit.

Schema mismatch #2: lore schema uses `body` + timestamps; war-prep snippets use `rationale` + `status`. Compat shim applied: body = rationale, status preserved as extra field, created/updated set to session end. Future lore snippets should include both for renderer compat until Codex views migrate.

Schema mismatch #3: schisms have two shapes in the tree — legacy {context, volumes, rejected, chosen, reason, canon_id} and v2 {title, proposal, pros, cons, resolution, references, status}. Applied v2 fields verbatim and back-filled legacy {rejected, reason} from {proposal, resolution} for renderer compat.

canon-inst-003 cluster-assignment left PENDING_SOVEREIGN with three candidates enumerated in the rationale (new Cluster C / attach to Cluster A under Cipher / Monument-class cluster-less like Command Center). Sovereign selects at ratification.
Watchtower structural class recognized provisionally in canon-inst-003 for the duration of War Time + Working Committee review. Book III amendment (if warranted) is post-war work.

Session-log handler spec adopted the legacy `{_snippet_version, session_log: [{op, data}]}` envelope over the `{snippet_type, operations}` envelope — keeps dispatcher changes to three `if` branches rather than a full rewrite; tolerant-reader bridge is future work.
Handler spec upgrades error handling from silent-skip to accumulate-and-warn — wartime telemetry cannot afford silently-dropped task_status updates.
Dashboard Phase 1 briefing notes Phase 1 is effectively pre-completed (v2.1 restoration landed pre-dawn); Nyx+Sovereign's Hour 0 move is verification + Phase 2 ingestion, not rebuild.

Invoicing briefing locks Phase 1 as polish-only (no new screens, no schema changes, ≤200 LOC per PR). Any deeper bug found during polish becomes its own branched task under Edict V (Capital Protection).
session_logs.handoff: Snippets 01–07 live on branch claude/war-time-chronicler-teVed (pending merge to main). Temple will reflect on merge. canon-inst-003 and session_log handler spec queued under docs/ for Sovereign/Orinth. Two new briefings live at docs/briefings/. This session_log also queued. Next War Tracker responsibilities: (a) monitor Temple dashboard as Builders post session_logs; (b) hand-merge session_log payloads into journal.json + campaigns.json until Orinth's handler ratifies; (c) write Hour 12 Chronicle at next rollover (first of six canonical checkpoints).

session_logs.summary: Review of sl-1-1 audit PR #3 — blocking feedback posted as COMMENT event (GitHub rejects self-account REQUEST_CHANGES; treat as changes-requested). Frame assessment: citations accurate, 'lie by omission' promotion over briefing's 'hardcoded' framing is correct, but the derived-store contract is incomplete. Abstraction assessment: under-indexed — three-state collapses _syncDisabled (local fault) into offline (network fault), the architecture formula never reads the onSnapshotsInSync drain it commits to adding, pending-count disjointness is undefined, and initial-boot (listener-not-yet-fired) state is unhandled. Five blockers plus five comment-level nits; no out-of-scope bugs filed.
decisions: Request changes (posted as COMMENT) on sproutlab PR #3; do not merge until blockers 1-5 resolved.
Answer Lyra review-ask #2 (_syncDisabled -> offline collapse): NO. Add a fourth state ('halted') with distinct copy and reload affordance; collapsing trades a pixel-lie for a semantic-lie.

Answer Lyra review-ask #3 (pending-count double-count): YES, risk is real. Resolve by disjointness contract (debounceTimers = pre-submit; hasPendingWrites across shadows = post-submit) or by leaning on onSnapshotsInSync as single source of truth.

Answer Lyra review-ask #1 (architecture contract soundness): NO. Formula must read the drain signal it lists as required (onSnapshotsInSync); must acknowledge sync.js:177 enablePersistence({synchronizeTabs:true}) — offline writes live in Firestore's IndexedDB queue, not in _syncDebounceTimers.
Flagged existing #syncToast (sync.js:970-999) as a second feedback channel the audit does not address; sl-1-2 must pick delete / unify-from-store / keep-with-divergence.
Flagged initial-boot lie: _syncReady listed as reusable but never used in derived equation; cold-start formula returns 'online' before any listener has fired.
session_logs.handoff: sl-1-1 blocked pending Lyra revision. Re-review queued for next push. Doctrine candidate flagged to Aurelius (not yet drafted): 'gap audits must use framework-provided ACK primitives (onSnapshotsInSync / hasPendingWrites) instead of hand-rolled counters when available.' No out-of-scope bugs filed. Issue #25 carried the session_log payload per briefing fallback path.

session_logs.summary: Phase-1 Builder session — anchor task sl-1-1 (charter revision r2), work spans sl-1-1 r2 + sl-1-2 r2/r3 (5-state model, toast retirement, _syncTrackWrite removal with {includeMetadataChanges} listeners, HR-3 scope-split to r3) + sl-1-3 r1 (offline badge additive, 108/2 diff, base retargeted to sl-1-2 branch). 7 local commits (force-pushed 54b5fe3 excluded from history); SHAs canonicalized at Sovereign squash-merge. loc_delta approximate; authoritative at squash-merge time.
decisions: sl-1-1 r2 charter revision addressing Cipher r1 verdict (5 architectural blockers)
Five-state sync model (replaces three-state) — green / syncing / amber / red / offline, disjoint
Toast retirement — #syncToast removed; offline badge (sl-1-3) replaces surface
_syncTrackWrite hand-rolled counter removed; onSnapshotsInSync + hasPendingWrites via {includeMetadataChanges} listeners (framework-provided ACK primitives per Cipher r1 insight)
HR-3 (data-action delegation) compliance fix scope-split to sl-1-2 r3 — kept separate from r2's architectural fixes for review clarity
sl-1-3 scope strictly badge-additive: styles.css + sync.js + template.html; core.js untouched
PR #5 (sl-1-3) base retargeted to sl-1-2 branch pre-commit — avoids r2 re-review contamination in sl-1-3 diff
session_logs.handoff: Phase-1 Builder session closed. All three tasks (sl-1-1, sl-1-2, sl-1-3) awaiting Cipher's combined r3/r1 review + Sovereign squash. No task-status flips until merge. Doctrine candidate (framework-ACK-primitives) tagged source: cipher-r1-review, status: queued-for-hour-12-chronicle.
